ucs.title: [LLM] APT-C-36 (Blind Eagle) staged loader: LOLBin fetch from public hosting → .NET utility process hollowing
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Blind Eagle / TAG-144 infection chain — a Spanish-language phishing payload uses mshta/PowerShell/WScript to pull a second stage from a public file-hosting / paste service (Bitbucket, Discord CDN, archive.org, paste.ee, GitHub raw, IPFS, trycloudflare tunnels) followed within seconds-to-minutes by execution of a .NET Framework LOLBin (RegAsm/RegSvcs/InstallUtil/aspnet_compiler/MSBuild) that AsyncRAT, Remcos and BitRAT loaders consistently hollow into. This combination is the actor's signature; the public-host plus .NET LOLBin chain has been documented across QiAnXin (2019),
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as fetch_time values(Web.url) as fetch_url values(Web.user) as user from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.app IN ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","curl.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe") AND (Web.url="*bitbucket.org*" OR Web.url="*discordapp.com*" OR Web.url="*discord.com*" OR Web.url="*cdn.discordapp.com*" OR Web.url="*archive.org*" OR Web.url="*paste.ee*" OR Web.url="*pastebin.com*" OR Web.url="*controlc.com*" OR Web.url="*textbin*" OR Web.url="*ipfs.io*" OR Web.url="*raw.githubusercontent.com*" OR Web.url="*trycloudflare.com*" OR Web.url="*workers.dev*" OR Web.url="*1024tera*" OR Web.url="*mediafire*" OR Web.url="*anonfiles*") by host Web.app | rename host as dest Web.app as fetcher_process | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count max(_time) as hollow_time values(Processes.process) as hollow_cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as hollow_parent values(Processes.process_name) as hollow_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("RegAsm.exe","RegSvcs.exe","InstallUtil.exe","aspnet_compiler.exe","MSBuild.exe","jsc.exe","csc.exe") AND Processes.process_path="*Microsoft.NET\\Framework*" by host | rename host as dest ] | eval delta=hollow_time-fetch_time | where delta>=0 AND delta<=900 | table fetch_time hollow_time delta dest user fetcher_process fetch_url hollow_proc hollow_parent hollow_cmd | sort - fetch_time
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 900;
let PublicHosters = dynamic(["bitbucket.org","discordapp.com","discord.com","cdn.discordapp.com","archive.org","paste.ee","pastebin.com","controlc.com","textbin.net","ipfs.io","raw.githubusercontent.com","githubusercontent.com","trycloudflare.com","workers.dev","mediafire.com","anonfiles.com","1024tera.com","mega.nz","bashupload.com"]);
let Fetchers = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","curl.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let StageFetch = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (Fetchers)
    | where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (PublicHosters)
    | project FetchTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              FetcherUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              FetcherProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              FetcherCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              StageUrl = RemoteUrl, StageIP = RemoteIP;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName in~ ("RegAsm.exe","RegSvcs.exe","InstallUtil.exe","aspnet_compiler.exe","MSBuild.exe","jsc.exe","csc.exe")
| where FolderPath has @"\Microsoft.NET\Framework"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner StageFetch on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (FetchTime .. FetchTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project FetchTime, HollowTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, FetchTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          FetcherProc, FetcherCmd, StageUrl, StageIP,
          HollowTarget = FileName, HollowCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          HollowParent = InitiatingProcessFileName, HollowParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by FetchTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 900;
let PublicHosters = dynamic(["bitbucket.org","discordapp.com","discord.com","cdn.discordapp.com","archive.org","paste.ee","pastebin.com","controlc.com","textbin.net","ipfs.io","raw.githubusercontent.com","githubusercontent.com","trycloudflare.com","workers.dev","mediafire.com","anonfiles.com","1024tera.com","mega.nz","bashupload.com"]);
let Fetchers = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","curl.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let StageFetch = ImWebSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | extend FetcherProc = tolower(tostring(split(coalesce(SrcUsername,""), "\\")[-1]))
    | where Url has_any (PublicHosters) or HttpHostName has_any (PublicHosters)
    | project FetchTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, SrcUsername, StageUrl = Url, StageHost = HttpHostName;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where TargetProcessName has_any ("RegAsm.exe","RegSvcs.exe","InstallUtil.exe","aspnet_compiler.exe","MSBuild.exe","jsc.exe","csc.exe")
| where TargetProcessName has @"\Microsoft.NET\Framework"
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName has_any (Fetchers)
| join kind=inner StageFetch on DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (FetchTime .. FetchTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project FetchTime, HollowTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, FetchTime),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine,
          StageUrl, StageHost,
          HollowTarget = TargetProcessName, HollowCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine
| order by FetchTime desc
ucs.rationale: Blind Eagle / APT-C-36 / TAG-144 has been documented since 2019 (QiAnXin) through 2025 (Check Point, Recorded Future) using a remarkably consistent loader chain: spearphish (often Colombian DIAN tax / judicial-summons lures) drops a script that fetches an obfuscated AsyncRAT, Remcos or BitRAT stage from a public service, then process-hollows into a .NET Framework LOLBin (RegAsm.exe is the dominant

ucs.title: [LLM] APT-C-36 (Blind Eagle) AsyncRAT/Remcos beacon to dynamic-DNS C2 from non-browser process
ucs.description: Surfaces non-browser processes egressing to dynamic-DNS providers (DuckDNS, No-IP, DDNS, Hopto, Sytes, Zapto, Dynu) — Blind Eagle's near-exclusive C2 infrastructure choice for their cracked AsyncRAT, Remcos and njRAT builds, repeatedly observed by Kaspersky (2024) and Check Point (2025) as `*.duckdns.org` and `*.ddns.net` callbacks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen dc(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as unique_dest_ips dc(All_Traffic.dest_port) as unique_ports values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as ports values(All_Traffic.app) as process from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_host="*.duckdns.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.ddns.net" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.hopto.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.sytes.net" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.zapto.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.no-ip.biz" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.no-ip.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.myftp.biz" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.myftp.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.dynu.net" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.servehttp.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.serveftp.com") AND All_Traffic.app!="chrome.exe" AND All_Traffic.app!="msedge.exe" AND All_Traffic.app!="firefox.exe" AND All_Traffic.app!="brave.exe" AND All_Traffic.app!="iexplore.exe" AND All_Traffic.app!="opera.exe" AND All_Traffic.app!="outlook.exe" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest_host All_Traffic.app | rename All_Traffic.* as * | where count>=5 | sort - count
ucs.kql:
let DynDnsTlds = dynamic([".duckdns.org",".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".sytes.net",".zapto.org",".no-ip.biz",".no-ip.org",".myftp.biz",".myftp.org",".dynu.net",".servehttp.com",".serveftp.com"]);
let Browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (DynDnsTlds)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (Browsers)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| summarize Connections = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            DistinctRemoteIPs = dcount(RemoteIP),
            DistinctPorts = dcount(RemotePort),
            SamplePort = any(RemotePort),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            ParentProc = any(InitiatingProcessParentFileName)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, RemoteUrl
| where Connections >= 5
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let DynDnsTlds = dynamic([".duckdns.org",".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".sytes.net",".zapto.org",".no-ip.biz",".no-ip.org",".myftp.biz",".myftp.org",".dynu.net",".servehttp.com",".serveftp.com"]);
let Browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe"]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(DstHostname)
| where DstHostname has_any (DynDnsTlds)
| extend ProcLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(coalesce(ActingProcessName,""),"\\")[-1]))
| where ProcLeaf !in~ (Browsers)
| where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
| summarize Connections = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DistinctRemoteIPs = dcount(DstIpAddr),
            DistinctPorts = dcount(DstPortNumber),
            SamplePort = any(DstPortNumber),
            SampleCmd = any(ActingProcessCommandLine)
            by DvcHostname, SrcUsername, ProcLeaf, DstHostname
| where Connections >= 5
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT-C-36 (Blind Eagle) RAT Beacon to Dynamic DNS Provider
id: 1f9c2e7a-3b4d-4e2a-9c8f-blindeagle0099
status: experimental
description: Detects non-browser Windows processes initiating outbound network connections to dynamic-DNS providers (DuckDNS, No-IP, DDNS, Hopto, Sytes, Zapto, Dynu) — the canonical C2 infrastructure for APT-C-36 / Blind Eagle / TAG-144 AsyncRAT, Remcos and njRAT campaigns targeting Latin America.
references:
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0099/
    - https://securelist.com/blindeagle-flying-high-with-latin-america/113830/
    - https://research.checkpoint.com/2025/blind-eagle-and-justice-for-all/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
    - attack.command_and_control
    - attack.t1568.002
    - attack.t1571
    - attack.t1071.001
    - attack.g0099
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_dyndns:
        DestinationHostname|endswith:
            - '.duckdns.org'
            - '.ddns.net'
            - '.hopto.org'
            - '.sytes.net'
            - '.zapto.org'
            - '.no-ip.biz'
            - '.no-ip.org'
            - '.myftp.biz'
            - '.myftp.org'
            - '.dynu.net'
            - '.servehttp.com'
            - '.serveftp.com'
    filter_browsers:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\chrome.exe'
            - '\msedge.exe'
            - '\firefox.exe'
            - '\brave.exe'
            - '\opera.exe'
            - '\iexplore.exe'
            - '\outlook.exe'
            - '\teams.exe'
    condition: selection_dyndns and not filter_browsers
falsepositives:
    - Sysadmin remote-access tools using DuckDNS/No-IP for home labs (allowlist by host/process)
    - Legitimate IoT or remote-monitoring agents
level: high
ucs.rationale: Across every public Blind Eagle report (QiAnXin 2019, Trend 2021, BlackBerry 2023, Kaspersky 2024, Check Point 2025, Recorded Future TAG-144 2025) the C2 destination is a dynamic-DNS subdomain — overwhelmingly `*.duckdns.org`, with `*.ddns.net` and `*.hopto.org` as supporting choices — because the actor relies on free, low-cost infrastructure they can rotate quickly. Pinning to dynamic-DNS TLDs al

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
